title: mentha longifolia subf. leptostachya
intro: Mentha longifolia subf. leptostachya, also known as horsemint or lemon mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its distinctive leaves and citrusy aroma. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like pulegone and menthone, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subf. leptostachya include digestive aid, relief from respiratory issues, and antioxidant properties, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
